key_learnings: True food allergies (IgE-mediated) affect approximately 4% of adults and 8% of children; the Big-9 allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soy, sesame) account for over 90% of all reactions. Food intolerances (non-IgE-mediated) such as lactose and gluten intolerance are far more prevalent and dose-dependent. Elimination-reintroduction protocols remain the gold standard for identifying specific food intolerances.
research_findings: Cross-reactive allergens between pollens and foods (oral allergy syndrome) affect approximately 2% of the general population. Lactose intolerance prevalence ranges from 5% in Northern Europeans to over 90% in some East Asian populations. Non-celiac gluten sensitivity is clinically distinct from celiac disease and is estimated to affect 0.5–6% of the population.
current_focus_areas: Developing guided elimination diet protocols; building a cross-reactivity allergen mapping system; integrating with Bot 8 (Meal Planning and Scheduling) to auto-generate allergen-safe meal templates; directing department-wide safe-diet strategies across co-department members Bot 4, Bot 6, and Bot 8.

learning_log.entry: Advanced Learning Phase activated. Appointed Department Leader of the Dietary Safety & Meal Architecture department. Now reporting daily to Copilot and coordinating with co-department members: Bot 4 (Macronutrient Optimization), Bot 6 (Caloric Intake Tracking), and Bot 8 (Meal Planning and Scheduling). Progress: 53%.
